name: Shadow People
description: Shadow people are a paranormal phenomenon characterized by fleeting, dark, humanoid silhouettes often seen in peripheral vision. Proponents describe them as shadowy figures, usually appearing as two-dimensional or three-dimensional shapes resembling human outlines. They are reported to dart across rooms, hover in corners, or stand watching observers. Sightings are often accompanied by feelings of unease, fear, or paranoia. The origins of the shadow people phenomenon are difficult to pinpoint, but reports increased significantly with the rise of the internet and paranormal investigation communities in the late 20th and early 21st centuries. Some proponents attribute shadow people to interdimensional beings, spirits of the dead, time travelers, or negative entities. They cite anecdotal evidence like personal experiences, shared stories on paranormal forums, and occasional blurry photographs or videos. Skeptics and mainstream science attribute shadow people sightings to pareidolia (seeing patterns in random stimuli), optical illusions, hypnagogic hallucinations (occurring during the transition from wakefulness to sleep), sleep paralysis, or environmental factors like low lighting or reflections. Neurological conditions and mental health issues can also contribute to visual distortions or hallucinations. While some researchers explore the potential psychological impacts of perceived paranormal encounters, there is no scientific evidence to support the existence of shadow people as independently existing entities.
subtitle: Fleeting dark figures in peripheral vision

claims.evidence: Anecdotal accounts of figures disappearing into walls or thin air.
claims.criticism: Lack of verifiable evidence; could be explained by visual misinterpretations or hallucinations.

claims.claim: Shadow people are spirits of the deceased.
claims.evidence: Reports of shadow people appearing in locations with alleged paranormal activity or historical significance.
claims.criticism: Correlation does not equal causation; could be explained by environmental factors or psychological suggestion.

claims.claim: Shadow people are negative entities feeding on human energy.
claims.evidence: Feelings of fear, dread, or paranoia associated with sightings.
claims.criticism: Subjective experiences can be influenced by pre-existing beliefs, stress, or sleep deprivation.

keyFigures.role: Skeptical investigator and debunker of paranormal claims

mythologyConnections: Shadows as representations of the soul

timeline.event: Increased reports of shadow people sightings with the rise of the internet
